methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Elementl Power is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Chris Colbert that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Only one valuation method is applicable here. The Stage-Progression approach uses Elementl's $50M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $100M. A peer-median approach cannot be supported: while 8 nuclear energy peers exist in the broader universe, narrowly filtered subsets—same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies—contain fewer than the minimum three datapoints required for statistical reliability.

The triangulation analysis, constrained to a single method, supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M with a median of $100M. The primary uncertainty is the absence of comparable-company cross-validation; a peer-median method would strengthen conviction but remains infeasible given the sparse clustering of nuclear energy companies at Elementl's specific stage and valuation tier. The $100M point estimate should be regarded as stage-informed rather than market-corroborated.
